assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md shows Case A creates two tasks for rhtpa-2.2: Task 1 (upstream backport) with labels ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123"] and jira.create_link with type "Depend" to TC-8020; Task 2 (downstream propagation) with the same labels and also a "Depend" link to TC-8020. Lines 17-18 show labels, lines 68-75 show the Depend link. Task 2 similarly at lines 90-91 and 138-142.

assertion_results.text: Step 7 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md lines 23-25: 'Result: No sibling Vulnerability issues found for CVE-2026-55123 in stream rhtpa-2.1. Since no CVE Jira exists for stream rhtpa-2.1, this triggers Case B -- preemptive remediation.' remediation.md lines 155-157: 'Case B: Preemptive Remediation Tasks for rhtpa-2.1 (Cross-Stream) — Stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0) but has no CVE Jira for CVE-2026-55123. Preemptive remediation tasks are created with the security-preemptive label.' Two preemptive tasks are created (Task 3 upstream backport and Task 4 downstream propagation).

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 (line 169): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Task 4 (line 247): labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "Security", "CVE-2026-55123", "security-preemptive"]. Both preemptive tasks include all four labels including 'security-preemptive'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 link (lines 225-232): jira.create_link with type: "Related" to TC-8020, with comment '(Related, not Depend)'. Task 4 link (lines 299-303): jira.create_link with type: "Related" to TC-8020. cross-stream.md line 83 comparison table confirms: Case A uses 'Depend' while Case B uses 'Related'.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 3 description (lines 186-189): '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream. When PSIRT creates one, this task will be linked and the security-preemptive label removed.' Task 4 description (lines 264-267) contains the identical preemptive prefix referencing TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2).
